content: Psychosis refers to a group of symptoms involving loss of contact with reality, including delusions (fixed false beliefs despite contrary evidence), hallucinations (sensory perceptions without external stimulus, auditory most common), disorganized thinking/speech (loose associations, word salad, tangentiality), and grossly disorganized/abnormal motor behavior (including catatonia). Psychosis occurs in schizophrenia spectrum disorders, mood disorders with psychotic features (bipolar, depression), substance-induced psychosis, medical conditions (delirium, dementia, neurological disorders), and brief psychotic disorder. First-episode psychosis typically emerges in late adolescence/early adulthood. Differential diagnosis critical as treatment varies by etiology.
